name: 1-navigate-to.spec.ts
description:
1. Navigate to Amazon Singapore homepage.
2. Wait for page load (networkidle).
3. Locate the search input using fallback selector

steps: Locate the search input using fallback selectors
Wait for results page to load (networkidle)
Locate and click the Filter (e.g., 'Price: Under $500') using fallback selectors
Wait for filtered results to load dynamically
Scroll into view if needed and verify product cards are visible
Confirm at least one product result is displayed
risks: Search input may have multiple or changing selectors
Price filter may load slowly or be rendered asynchronously
Results page and filtered results load dynamically, causing timing issues
Product cards may be rendered below the viewport, requiring scrolling
Selectors may change due to A/B testing or UI updates
Networkidle may not guarantee all dynamic content is loaded
healingStrategies: Use selector fallback chains for search input and filter elements
Implement retry logic or increased timeout for slow-loading filters
Use waitForSelector with visible:true in addition to networkidle for dynamic content
Use scrollIntoViewIfNeeded before interacting with or verifying product cards
Leverage text-based selectors where possible to reduce dependency on class names
Add logging and screenshots on failure for easier diagnosis of flakiness
